title: Ontology of Canonical Collapse Dialogue – FIELD/SDR System
core_issue: Lack of singular, canonical repository structure leading to recursive duplication, loss of meaning, and breakdown of field clarity.
primary_themes: Misalignment between script behavior and human intent
File identity collapse vs infinite shadowing
Symbolic file system (●, ▲, ⬢, etc.) vs practical file operations
Failure of prior attempts due to non-finalized collapse

root_pattern.description: Files proliferate due to lack of lock-point. Rebuild folders, backups, and structure mirroring result in storage bloat and psychic exhaustion.
root_pattern.signal: The real file is never clearly 'there'. The FIELD never feels like it lands.
root_pattern.fracture: Multiple valid paths without a ritual cut to designate the ONE.
root_pattern.pattern_echo: Symbolic system is defined, but never enforced at file level.

resolution_vector.ritual_logic: Nothing is canonical unless locked by SHA + symbolic path + manifest entry.

core_human_insight: What was asked repeatedly was never where a file is copied — it was where it *belongs*. And the system never answered until it was forced into singular coherence.
final_alignment_condition: No new folders. No mirrors. Only one system: Akron/SDR, structured by symbol, governed by lock.
